name: I B E W 175
description_short: IBEW 175 Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution serving union members and their families since 1961, offering competitive loan rates, savings accounts, and digital banking services.
description_long:
IBEW 175 Federal Credit Union has been operating since October 14, 1961, as a not-for-profit, member-owned financial cooperative. The credit union was established to serve members of the International Brotherhood of Electrical Workers (IBEW) Local 175 and their families, providing accessible financial services to this community. The organization is federally chartered and NCUA-insured, operating under the regulatory framework that governs credit unions in the United States.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including auto loans (as low as 4.00% APR), camper loans (6.00% APR), signature loans (12.00% APR), and various savings and share accounts. Members have access to eStatements at no cost, online and mobile banking available 24/7/365, and a Skip-a-Payment program that allows borrowers to temporarily pause loan payments during financial hardship. The institution provides financial calculators to help members understand loan costs and savings growth, and maintains a loan application process designed to help members finance vehicles and other assets competitively.

IBEW 175 FCU distinguishes itself through its union affiliation and member-centric approach, offering rates and terms specifically competitive for its membership base. The credit union emphasizes personal service and community, marketing membership as providing "comprehensive financial products, competitive rates and great personal service." The free mobile apps for iOS and Android, combined with 24/7 account access, position the credit union as technologically accessible. Additionally, the Skip-a-Payment program demonstrates flexibility in member hardship situations.

However, the website contains limited detail about eligibility requirements, specific membership criteria, or account features beyond basic descriptions. The audit department contact address in Acworth, Georgia, and references to "membership qualification" suggest membership may be restricted to specific populations rather than open to all consumers. The rates displayed are "as of 08/01/2025" and explicitly subject to change, and loan approval depends on individual creditworthiness and other standard underwriting criteria. For those outside the union membership base, this credit union may not be accessible.
services: Auto loans with rates as low as 4.00% APR
Signature loans at competitive rates (as low as 12.00% APR)
Share savings accounts and deposit products
Mobile banking apps for iOS and Android
Skip-a-Payment program for loan payment relief
Financial calculators for loan and savings planning
Competitive savings rates for member deposits
pros: Auto loans as low as 4.00% APR, significantly below national averages for credit union rates
Camper/recreational vehicle loans at 6.00% APR, a specialized loan product not all credit unions offer
Free eStatements and 24/7/365 online and mobile banking access included with membership
Skip-a-Payment program allowing temporary payment relief during financial hardship
No-cost financial calculators to help members understand loan costs before applying
Member-owned structure means any profits benefit members through better rates and lower fees
Established 64-year track record serving the same membership community since 1961
cons: Membership eligibility appears restricted to IBEW Local 175 members and their families, limiting accessibility to general consumers
Website provides minimal detail about specific membership requirements, account fees, or eligibility criteria
Loan rates are subject to change and approval depends on credit profile, income verification, and standard underwriting
Limited loan product information—website does not disclose maximum loan amounts, term lengths, or rates for signature loans beyond the floor rate of 12.00% APR
best_for: IBEW Local 175 union members seeking competitive auto and vehicle financing
Credit union members prioritizing 24/7 digital banking and mobile app access
Borrowers experiencing temporary financial hardship who need flexible payment options
Union households seeking member-owned financial institutions with community focus
diagnosis: IBEW 175 Federal Credit Union is best for current and prospective members of IBEW Local 175 seeking competitive vehicle financing and union-member-focused financial services with strong digital banking capabilities. The primary caveat is that membership is restricted to union members and their families, making it inaccessible to the general public; eligibility requirements should be confirmed directly with the credit union before applying.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
